title: AI — Consumption & Cost
description: Token throughput and spend — the FinOps view. Token counters are live. **Cost is still not meaningful**: computedCost requires configured cost rates, and LibreChat's Agents API returns an all-zero usage object for headless API-key calls (#38), so usage is frequently null at the source. LiteLLM (#102) is what makes it authoritative.

panels.title: What is and is not trustworthy here

options.content:
**Tokens: live.** `nexus_scheduler_run_tokens_total{model,type}` is populated from the usage LibreChat reports, labelled by the model that actually served the call.

**Cost: not yet trustworthy.** Two independent reasons:

1. **Cost rates** must be configured per agent, or `computeCost` returns null and nothing is counted.
2. **#102 — LiteLLM as the gateway.** LibreChat's Agents API returns an **all-zero usage object** (#38) for headless API-key calls, so token counts are frequently `null` at the source. Tokens are recorded only when usage is actually reported — a zero would read as "this run was free" rather than "we were never told".

Until both land, spend panels stay empty **by design** rather than showing a confidently wrong number.

panels.title: Tokens/sec by model
panels.description: Labelled by the serving model, never by agentId/jobId/userId — those are user-created and unbounded (#108 is the same mistake in the API route label).

panels.title: Spend (24h)
panels.description: Requires nexus_scheduler_run_cost_total. Meaningful only once LiteLLM (#102) makes usage authoritative.

panels.title: Cost per successful run
panels.description: Unit economics: spend divided by successful runs. The number that tells you whether a model/prompt change actually paid off.

panels.title: LiteLLM spend by key/model
panels.description: LiteLLM exposes native Prometheus metrics for per-key/model spend, tokens, latency and failures — the authoritative source once #102 lands. It also enforces hard budgets, turning this from a cost *report* into a cost *control*.

panels.title: Tokens per run (avg, by model)
panels.description: Total tokens divided by successful runs, per model. The unit-economics number: it moves when a prompt or model changes, and is the cheapest early warning that a prompt has quietly grown. Written with on() group_left(): the numerator carries a model label and the denominator does not, so a plain division silently matches nothing.
